name: Java
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Java as a competency should be able to / is capable of writing and debugging production-ready applications using core Java constructs (loops, conditionals, generics, annotations, Collections Framework) while applying OOP principles (inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, abstraction). They demonstrate moderate concurrency skills (Threads, Runnables, Executors) with a basic grasp of the Java Memory Model, follow SOLID principles to avoid code smells, and effectively use frameworks like Spring (Spring Boot, MVC, Data) or Jakarta EE for building web applications, REST APIs, and microservices, integrating common logging (SLF4J, Log4j) and security (Spring Security). They employ ORM libraries (JPA/Hibernate) to map entities to relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle) with an understanding of eager vs. lazy loading and manage environment-based configurations using build tools like Maven and Gradle. They containerize applications with Docker, have some exposure to cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure), and set up CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions) for automated builds, tests, and deployments. They write unit and integration tests (JUnit, TestNG) with mocking (Mockito), measure coverage with Jacoco or SonarQube, and practice basic TDD or BDD if required. They troubleshoot performance issues using profiling tools (VisualVM, JDK Mission Control, YourKit), refine inefficient queries, and optimize concurrency and data structures.

name: Java - Distributed Systems Concurrency
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Java Distributed Systems should be able to implement advanced Kafka patterns (consumer group management, partition strategies, exactly-once semantics with transactions, manual offset management, dead letter topics, consumer lag monitoring), configure advanced RabbitMQ setups (exchange types — direct/topic/fanout/headers, message acknowledgment modes, prefetch tuning, publisher confirms, dead letter exchanges), use Redis for distributed locking (Redisson/RedisLock), pub/sub patterns, cache invalidation strategies (cache-aside, write-through, write-behind), and Redis Streams, implement distributed transaction patterns (saga choreography vs orchestration, compensating transactions, outbox pattern, eventual consistency), design inter-service communication using gRPC basics, REST vs messaging trade-offs, and API gateway patterns, apply resilience patterns with Resilience4j (advanced circuit breaker config, retry with exponential backoff and jitter, bulkhead, rate limiting, timeout management, fallback strategies), work with distributed coordination (leader election basics, distributed locks, service discovery with Eureka/Consul, Spring Cloud Config), implement event-driven architecture patterns (event sourcing basics, CQRS, domain events, idempotent consumers, deduplication strategies), set up distributed observability (OpenTelemetry/Zipkin/Jaeger tracing, correlation IDs, centralized logging with ELK, Micrometer/Prometheus metrics), and handle deployment concerns (horizontal scaling, stateless service design, graceful shutdown with in-flight message handling).
